MINI MINUTES:
No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

An Order to Appear will be issued to Babrbara Lewis and the Attorney Jonathan Nightingale to appear on April 28, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why she should not be removed as trustee. The future accounting hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Musto. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Sep-30-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Marilyn Young, in court, for Barbara Ann Lewis, Trustee.

The court begins by advising counsel that a revised accounting is considered an amendment. The court then confirms that it is waiving notice for the late submission of a declaration which included language regarding an amended accounting. The court then confirms with counsel that this accounting is the eleventh (11th) accounting but that she has submitted it as her second (2nd) accounting as this is the second (2nd) accounting submitted in this new trust case number. The court then queries Ms. Young regarding the request to distribute funds from the Charles Schwab sweep account. Ms. Young confirms that it had been discovered that dividends had not been issued and did not want the funds distributed without seeking the court's authorization to do so. Ms. The court then advises Ms. Young that many of her time entries appear to be clerical in nature which are typically not approved by the court and that the request for twenty-four (24) hours spent on the accounting seems excessive. Ms. Young provides oral testimony supporting her request including being a sole practicing attorney without staff to support her and that had she hired a paralegal to assist her, the fee request would likely have been much higher but understands if the court reduces her fees. The court reminds Ms. Young that moving forward, she should comply with the SFLR regarding submission of properly formatted timesheets.

The Accounting Petition is submitted.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Marilyn Young appeared by telephone for Barbara Lewis (Trustee).

Ms. Young requested a two to three-week continuance to complete the accounting and get it on file. Status on filing of the second accounting is continued to April 26, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

First Account Current And Report Of Trustee Under Will And Petition For Allowance Of Trustee'S And A
Off Calendar Sep-16-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Marilyn Young for Barbara Lewis (Trustee).

The Court raised the issue of the number of hours spent responding to the Probate Examiner's notes. Ms. Young stated that the Trustee had been gone for a number of years, that the Examiner had a number of questions relating to the 9th accounting, that it took a number of hours to respond, and that all of the questions have been answered and resolved. Ms. Young also stated that none of the issues in the 9th accounting were brought in this present accounting, that everyone has received a copy of the petition and have not objected, that all of the Beneficiaries are informed, that the Trustee has not done the accounting, but has supplied all of the information and that the Trustee has done everything else. In addition, Ms. Young stated that the Trustee manages everything and keeps track of everything, that the taxes are being paid, that the Trustee acts right away when she finds that something is wrong, that the Trustee is organized and has materials ready to go when asked, and that the Trustee saves Counsel a number of hours. The Court informed Ms. Young that going forward, the Trustee must submit timesheets. The First Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
